advantages: Unique experiment IDs make every training run retrievable—params, metrics, and artifacts stay addressable months later.
Tags and filters scale comparison across dozens of runs without manual spreadsheet tracking.
Model registries separate exploration (many runs) from production (few approved versions) with clear promotion stages.
Lineage from registry version to run ID supports audits, rollbacks, and regulatory traceability.
disadvantages: Over-tagging or inconsistent tag names creates noise; without conventions, search becomes unreliable.
Registry overhead is unnecessary for throwaway prototypes—full versioning can slow early exploration.
Comparing runs is misleading if metric names, eval splits, or data versions differ across IDs.
Centralized tracking adds dependency on server uptime, access control, and storage costs for artifacts.
